title: Unrestricted File Upload - PHP Web Shell Detection and Remediation

steps.message: Immediately isolate and quarantine 'shell.php.jpg' from the web directory. Check for any other files with double extensions or anomalous naming conventions as referenced in WAHH 7.5.3.
Query logs for any execution attempts targeting the uploaded shell. Look for unusual error messages or status codes (304, 500) that might indicate script injection attempts (WAHH 7.6.2).

Remediate the vulnerability in the source code. Implement strict server-side validation that does not rely on client-side checks (WAHH Chapter 19). Replicate validation logic to check for byte patterns and enforce a strict whitelist of extensions, preventing bypasses like Shift-JIS or UTF-8 encoding exploits.

Review the forensics report and the code patch. Verify that the file upload functionality is now restricted to non-executable directories and that least-privilege OS accounts are being used for the application tier (WAHH Mitigations).
Notify leadership of the incident resolution and the steps taken to harden the shared infrastructure against further command injection or file access flaws.
